findings.observation: Model, Target, Factor, and Requirement each have a YAML schema block stating shape, requiredness (Required/Recommended/Optional), cardinality (e.g. requirement MUST declare exactly one assessment as a single non-empty scalar; ratingScale at least two levels), and defaults; an implementer could build a parser and an author write a valid file from these.

Malformed/omitted handling is stated: YAML that does not conform to the documented shapes is not a valid QUALITY.md; null/empty optional values are treated as absent; missing/empty/list-valued assessment is invalid.
Body is OPTIONAL with no required section set; a conforming tool MUST preserve unrecognized body content; recommended sections are enumerated as non-normative.

Gap: the spec defers the authoritative structural schema to a typed declaration in the implementation ('reconcile in favor of the documented format here'), slightly qualifying 'build from the spec alone'.
Gap: no in-file format-version field or stated forward-evolution path for QUALITY.md files; the document is versioned '0.1 — Draft' but file versioning is unspecified.

rationale: The frontmatter and body are specified completely enough to parse and author from, including malformed/omitted handling — satisfying the requirement. Held below outstanding by two edge gaps: reliance on an implementation schema as authoritative, and no specified file-versioning path.
